policy: Paper baseline rows using original paper method names must be produced by official or paper-linked source code, with URL, commit, license, environment, adapter, and config recorded.

sources.paper_name: Trajectory Anomaly Detection with By-Design Complementary Detectors
sources.repository_handling: Use as an external checkout for experiments. Do not vendor the third-party source into this repository unless explicit license or permission text is recorded with the copied code.
sources.notes: User confirms the paper permits using the code. Use this repository directly for experiments through an external checkout and record commit/config. Adapter exports <dict_name>_evaluation_gps.pkl plus sidecar/manifest and converts external score CSV/JSON/JSONL to benchmark score JSONL. Do not vendor source into this repository unless an explicit LICENSE or permission text is included.

sources.paper_name: Towards Physics-informed Diffusion for Anomaly Detection in Trajectories
sources.repository_handling: Prefer external checkout or pinned submodule after license verification. Keep generated checkpoints, results, and adapted data outside git.
sources.notes: Official code expects one flattened numeric trajectory per CSV row and emits trajectory_id,anomaly_score. The inspected train/detect scripts have a CPU/CUDA diffusion tensor initialization-order issue on GPU; experiments should record whether they used the original scripts directly or an external glue runner that preserves official Pi-DPM modules while constructing DiffusionModel after moving the model to the selected device.

sources.paper_name: Trajectory Anomaly Detection with Language Models
sources.repository_handling: External checkout or pinned submodule is allowed under MIT. Vendoring is allowed only if the MIT license text is copied with the third-party source.
sources.notes: Repository README identifies it as the SIGSPATIAL 2024 LM-TAD PyTorch implementation and GitHub reports MIT license. Adapter exports neutral trajectory token sequences plus manifest/vocab and converts official score CSV/TSV/JSON/JSONL to benchmark score JSONL. The inspected official loaders are Porto/POL-specific, so an external-checkout custom dataset loader is still required before reporting official LM-TAD results.

sources.paper_name: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy
sources.repository_handling: External checkout is allowed. Vendoring is allowed only if the MIT license text is copied with the third-party source.
sources.notes: The runner converts FusionTrack trajectories or group-object windows into fixed-length multivariate time series, uses the official model and association-discrepancy scoring components, and records the adapter aggregation config. The official code has CUDA assumptions in the attention module, so GPU execution is required for this adapter.

sources.paper_name: DCdetector: Dual Attention Contrastive Representation Learning for Time Series Anomaly Detection
sources.repository_handling: Do not vendor source into this repository unless license or permission text is recorded. Use only as an external checkout for experiments.
sources.notes: The runner converts FusionTrack samples into fixed-length multivariate time series and uses the official DCdetector module and dual-attention contrastive loss. The external runner supplies a minimal tkinter._flatten compatibility fallback when tkinter is unavailable.

sources.paper_name: TranAD: Deep Transformer Networks for Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series Data
sources.repository_handling: External checkout is allowed. Vendoring is allowed only if the BSD-3-Clause license text is copied with the third-party source.
sources.notes: The runner uses the official TranAD class and training/scoring loss. It isolates the official import-time argparse side effect, stubs unused dgl imports because GDN is not used, and wraps old Transformer layer forward signatures for current PyTorch compatibility.
